verses.meaning: I offer my obeisances to the Supreme Lord, whose form is the embodiment of eternal bliss and knowledge.
His glittering earrings swing to and fro as He flees from Mother Yashoda, who chases Him with a stick.
He is crying and rubbing His eyes again and again with His two lotus hands.
His eyes are filled with fear, and the necklace on His neck trembles as He breathes heavily. I worship that Damodar, bound by devotion.
By such pastimes, He drowns the residents of Gokula in pools of ecstatic bliss.
He reveals to the devotees who know His desires that He is conquered by their love. I worship Him hundreds of times.
O Lord, I do not desire liberation, nor do I desire any other benediction from You.
May this form of Yours as a cowherd boy forever remain in my heart. What need have I for anything else?
May this lotus face of Yours, surrounded by dark curling locks, and kissed repeatedly by Mother Yashoda,
With Your lips red as bimba fruits, remain in my heart. I have no desire for millions of other gains.
O Lord Damodar, O infinite Vishnu! O Master, please be gracious upon me who is drowning in an ocean of sorrows.
Shower me with the rain of Your merciful glance, and accept this ignorant soul. Please become visible to my eyes.
Just as You freed the two sons of Kubera who were bound as trees, and made them Your devotees,
Similarly, please grant me Your own loving devotion. I have no desire for liberation, O Damodar!
Obeisances to Your rope which binds You, shining with brilliant effulgence. Obeisances to Your belly, the abode of the universe.
Obeisances to Radhika, Your beloved. Obeisances to You, O Lord of infinite pastimes!
